prompt: Add error boundary component with error reporting

turns.content:
<task>
Add a React error boundary component that catches rendering errors and reports them.
</task>

arguments.query: React error boundary TypeScript class component getDerivedStateFromError componentDidCatch

turns.content:
1. React Error Boundaries - https://react.dev/reference/react/Component#catching-rendering-errors-with-an-error-boundary
   Class component with static getDerivedStateFromError and componentDidCatch.

arguments.title: React Error Boundary pattern
arguments.content:
Must be a class component (no hooks equivalent yet).
static getDerivedStateFromError(error): returns {hasError: true}
componentDidCatch(error, errorInfo): for logging/reporting
errorInfo.componentStack has the component tree trace
Wrap in render(): if (this.state.hasError) return fallback UI

turns.content: Finding recorded: React Error Boundary pattern

arguments.message: React error boundaries must be class components (no hooks equivalent yet). I'll create a reusable ErrorBoundary component with a customizable fallback UI and error reporting callback. Should it attempt auto-recovery (retry button) or just show the fallback?

arguments.summary: Created reusable ErrorBoundary component with retry button, customizable fallback, and onError callback for error reporting. TypeScript typed.
arguments.final_answer: ErrorBoundary component created with retry button, custom fallback prop, and onError callback for reporting. Wrap any component tree to catch rendering errors gracefully.
